claim: MCPs are flaky, face security vulnerabilities, and are hard to scale; most enterprises use fewer than single-digit MCPs due to observability, access control, and security issues
actionable: Before scaling MCPs in enterprise, invest in observability, access control infrastructure, and security hardening—don't rely on decentralized MCP deployments.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: Enterprise MCP challenges form a 'three-headed hydra': observability (who uses what, how often), access control (role-based tool scoping), and security (server verification, data exfiltration prevention)
actionable: Address all three challenges simultaneously with centralized infrastructure—security teams cannot audit MCPs individually at scale.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: MCPs designed for enterprise decentralization require establishing a single 'root of trust'—one blessed platform—to enable teams to safely build their own MCP servers
actionable: Implement a gateway as the single trusted intermediary between all agents and MCPs; security teams approve the gateway once, not every MCP.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: MCP gateways (middleware layer) abstract auth, access control, observability, routing, tunneling, and credential management—freeing teams to focus solely on business logic
actionable: Build or adopt a gateway that handles OAuth, roles, tunneling, and CLI tooling so domain teams (legal, sales, etc.) can develop MCPs independently.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: A gateway enables multiple surfaces (Claude web, Claude API, Cloud Core) to use the same MCP without reconfiguration—MCPs integrate once and work everywhere
actionable: Use a gateway to abstract surface differences so new platforms (Opus, Sonnet, future clients) automatically inherit all existing MCP integrations.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: Gateways enable delegated identity for agents (not just users) and role-based access scoped to teams, users, or agents—critical for future agentic systems
actionable: Design identity and access control for agents to be distinct from users; MCPs should know whether they're called by a user-agent or autonomous agent.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: Decoupling agent harness from data layer via gateways allows teams to choose which agents stay in-house vs. external without changing MCP integrations
actionable: Invest in gateway infrastructure now to remain flexible as agent deployment models evolve—the gateway becomes your invariant, agents and data layers are variables.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic

claim: Faster iteration through gatewayed MCPs: teams can change MCP logic without repeated security reviews if the gateway maintains boundaries and audit trails
actionable: Use gateway enforcement (not repeated human reviews) to speed MCP iteration cycles—security is standardized, not case-by-case.
source_title: Gateways are All You Need — Karan Sampath, Anthropic
